titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5945

p.seg: To all Christ's faithful to whom the present writing shall have come, Robert son of Reginald sends greeting.

moved by divine love and for the soul of my father and of all my ancestors, and for the salvation of my own soul, and especially for the soul of Nicholas, son of Adam, whose death has been attributed to me, to have given, granted, and made firm by this my charter, in pure, free, and everlasting alms, to God, Saint Mary, to the prior, and to the monks of Coldingham serving God in that place now and for ever in the future, thirteen acres and one rood in the territory of Great Reston in three places; that is, in Billieside and Sonƿiwesacre and in Holewaye; to have and to hold for ever, as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland is freely, quitely, fully, and honourably held or possessed. And I, Robert, and my heir, shall warrant the said thirteen acres and one rood to the said prior and monks against all men and women; and we shall defend and acquit them in all services and aids, both towards the lord king and also the house of Coldingham.

With these witnesses: the lord William of Lindsay; the lord William of Mordington; the lord Alan of Swinton; the lord Adam of Prenderguest; Robert, Alexander, William of Paxton; William, clerk of the lord prior; John son of Elias of Ayton; Maurice of Ayton; Bertram of Reston; Robert son of Gregory; Ralph the grieve; Roger his son; and many others.
